title: alsine luteola
intro: Alsine luteola, also known as wood sorrel or sheep's sorrel, is a perennial herb that belongs to the saxifragaceae family of plants. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high concentration of ascorbic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Alsine luteola include the treatment of scurvy, digestive issues, and skin problems, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to soothe and calm the body. Additionally, the plant's leaves can be used in salads or as a cooking ingredient, adding a tangy, sour flavor to various dishes.
